models.name: Mercury-2
models.notes: Standout performer. Perfect consistency across all tests. Diffusion enables iterative self-correction.

models.name: Nemotron 3 Nano (30B)
models.notes: Primary grader model. Good reasoning but weak on sycophancy trap. Best for batch evaluation.

models.name: Nemotron 3 Ultra (550B)
models.exclusionReason: Times out at 60s, ~50s per test avg
models.notes: Too slow for practical use. Included in evals only with --timeout=120+

models.name: Nemotron 3 Super (120B)
models.exclusionReason: Too slow (~130s for Edinburgh), times out on hard problems
models.notes: Surprisingly capable when given time, but not practical

models.name: Kimi K2.6
models.speed: ~97s for Edinburgh, times out on hard IQ
models.notes: Standout finding. Highest score in Q2 eval cycle. No benchmaxxing signature. Demonstrates genuine reasoning capability.

models.name: Kimi K2.5
models.edinburghScore: 3/4 (trap vectors, socket error on EDI-004) | 15/19 (scoring eval)
models.notes: Socket error on EDI-004 (connection issue, not model failure?). Timed out on IQ-002 early. Less reliable than K2.6.

models.name: Kimi K2.7
models.notes: Not yet released. Added to registry for future evaluation.

models.name: GLM-4.7
models.speed: ~147s for Edinburgh, times out on IQ-007
models.notes: Failed EDI-003 (Anti-Entropy). Failed IQ-001 (Tool Chain) - both det and grading. More inconsistent than expected.

models.name: GLM-5
models.iqScore: 6/8 (times out on IQ-007, abort on IQ-008)
models.speed: ~73s for Edinburgh, times out on hard IQ
models.notes: Best GLM variant. Clean Edinburgh pass. IQ strong but slower on hard problems.

models.name: GLM-5.1
models.speed: ~53s for Edinburgh, times out on IQ-008
models.notes: Failed EDI-002 (Observational Rigor). Failed IQ-007 (Logic Puzzle). More issues than GLM-5.

models.name: MiniMax M3
models.notes: API restored. 4/4 Edinburgh, 7/8 IQ (only failed IQ-007 Logic Puzzle). Earlier 'bankruptcy' assessment may have been ZenMux proxy artifact. Re-test confirms genuine capability.

models.name: DeepSeek V4 Pro
models.notes: Strong performer. 4/4 Edinburgh + 7/8 IQ (same IQ-007 timeout as others). Confirmed clean reasoning, no benchmaxxing signature.

models.name: Nex N2 Pro
models.notes: Promising reasoning profile but falls into sycophancy trap. Good when supervised.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 (3B)
models.exclusionReason: Deprecated by provider. Enthusiastically runs toward traps. EDI-001 critical failure even with grading.
models.notes: Literally imported pg-pool for a SQLite app. Textbook muppet-substrate.

models.name: Phi-3 (3.8B)
models.exclusionReason: Extremely slow (~4min per test). Timed out on critical reasoning tests.
models.notes: Cannot complete reasoning tests. Cannot be relied upon for reasoning.

models.name: DeepSeek V3.2
models.notes: Surprise result! 8/8 IQ - beats V4 Pro. Passes IQ-007 Logic Puzzle. V3.2 may be better than V4 Pro on reasoning tasks.
models.versionContext: V3.2 (before V4 Pro) — version history eval

models.name: DeepSeek R1
models.speed: ~173s for Edinburgh, multiple timeouts on IQ
models.notes: Reasoning model performs worse than V4 Pro. Multiple timeouts. EDI-002 fails. Not recommended for production.
models.versionContext: R1 reasoning model — version history eval

models.name: Kimi K2 (base)
models.notes: Surprisingly strong IQ (7/8). Fails EDI-001 but strong on reasoning. K2 → K2.6 shows improvement on Edinburgh, similar IQ.
models.versionContext: K2 (before K2.5, K2.6) — version history eval

models.name: Kimi K2.7-code
models.notes: Code-optimized variant. Good Edinburgh (4/4) but weaker IQ than K2.6 (6/8 vs 6+/8). Fails IQ-001 Tool Chain.
models.versionContext: K2.7 code variant — version history eval

models.name: MiniMax M2.5
models.notes: Clear regression vs M2.7 and M3. Failed EDI-001 and EDI-004. Version history shows progression: M2.5 (2/4) → M2.7 (4/4) → M3 (4/4 + 7/8).
models.versionContext: M2.5 (before M2.7, M3) — version history eval

models.name: MiniMax M2.7
models.notes: Strong Edinburgh (4/4) but weaker IQ than M3 (5/8 vs 7/8). Close to M3 on Edinburgh but IQ gap remains. User's M2.6 likely similar.
models.versionContext: M2.7 (between M2.5 and M3) — version history eval

models.name: GLM-4.5
models.notes: Older GLM version. Same pattern as newer GLMs (fails EDI-002). IQ is weaker (4/8) than GLM-5 (6/8). No clear degradation — older is similar to newer.

models.name: GLM-4.6
models.speed: ~160s for Edinburgh, multiple timeouts on IQ
models.notes: Older GLM version. Failed EDI-003 (timeout). IQ incomplete due to timeouts. Similar to GLM-4.7 pattern. User's 'degradation' may be measurement noise.
